name: FIU

confRecord.note: AAC Championship Final â€” lost to FAU 3-2. Regular season 4th (4-3 conf). Strong run.

notable: Michael Appiah â€” 2025 R3 Inter Miami
14 total MLS draft picks program history
proPlayers.draftRank: 14 all-time MLS picks; Inter Miami direct pipeline

degreeTitle: B.S. in Sport and Exercise Science (STEM)
acuAlignNote: Very strong â€” STEM-designated degree with anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, exercise prescription, resistance training, sport psych, and internship. FIU PT dept has DPT articulation for graduates.
soccerLevel: AAC â€” Elite (5 conf titles in 4 years)

gpa.note: FIU is very accessible for international athletes. GPA 2.8 is comfortable for admission.

fin.internationalNote: FIU out-of-state COA is ~$40k/yr (2025-26). Tuition is only ~$19k but Miami living costs push total to ~$40k. Still the most affordable D1 option in the guide by a significant margin. Athletic scholarship can cover nearly full tuition cost. Note: FIU Board of Trustees considering out-of-state fee increase for Fall 2026 â€” confirm with admissions.

culture.vibe: Urban Miami campus with strong Latin American cultural influence. FIU is one of the most diverse universities in the USA â€” over 50% Hispanic student body. Miami energy permeates everything. Students are ambitious, fashion-forward, and globally connected.
culture.thingsToDo: South Beach (20 min), Wynwood Arts District, Bayside Marketplace, Everglades day trips, Miami Heat/Marlins/Dolphins games, Calle Ocho festival, Little Havana restaurants, Fort Lauderdale (30 min), Key West (3.5hr drive).
culture.socialScene: Highly international. Spanish is widely spoken. Many students work part-time in Miami's hospitality and sport industry. Soccer culture is dominant â€” Inter Miami is 5 minutes away. Late-night lifestyle. Warm year-round.
culture.olivierMatch: â˜…â˜…â˜…â˜…â˜… Miami is arguably the most cosmopolitan US city match to Sydney's multicultural energy. FIU's Latin-influenced campus culture is vibrant and welcoming to Australians. Great lifestyle value.

facilityDetails.stadium: FIU Soccer Stadium â€” 2,000 capacity, FieldTurf surface, floodlit, AAC broadcast-ready. Adjacent to campus Athletic Complex. Inter Miami CF HQ is 5 minutes drive.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 FieldTurf practice fields at soccer complex. Access to additional FIU Athletics fields. GPS tracking zones installed 2022. Rebounders and training goal infrastructure throughout.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: FIU Athletics Performance Center â€” rebuilt 2021 with $4.2M investment. 14,000 sq ft. Olympic platforms (8), power racks, cable systems, Keiser resistance machines. Dedicated performance coaches for Olympic sports. Cryotherapy unit, normatec compression, HRV monitoring via Whoop integration.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: FIU Department of Kinesiology & Health Sciences â€” STEM-designated program with dedicated labs: exercise physiology lab (VO2 max, metabolic testing), biomechanics lab (3D capture, force plates), sport psychology suite. GPS tracking (Statsports) for all training sessions. Video analysis via Hudl with individual player dashboards.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: FIU Athletics Medicine â€” dedicated certified athletic trainer for soccer. University physician on contract. Miami sports medicine network access â€” Jackson Memorial Hospital, Baptist Health Sports Medicine. FIU Health Sciences campus shares facilities for advanced rehabilitation.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: FIU Kinesiology is STEM-designated â€” students can access research funding and graduate school pipelines not available in non-STEM exercise science degrees. Biodynamics Laboratory, Human Performance Laboratory. Pre-health advising formally embedded in kinesiology advising.
facilityDetails.extras: Proximity to Inter Miami CF training facility (5 min drive) â€” players have visited for training observation. Miami sports industry network: Heat, Marlins, Miami FC all accessible. FIU Recreation Center (195,000 sq ft â€” one of the largest in Florida). Miami climate means outdoor training 12 months/year.
facilityDetails.note: FIU's 2021 Athletics Performance Center rebuild is significant â€” it's better than many older D1 facilities. The STEM designation on the kinesiology degree and Inter Miami proximity are the standout differentiators at this price point.

coach.name: Kyle Russell
coach.title: Head Coach (7th season 2026)
coach.profile: 7th season at FIU 2026. Announced youngest HC in 2021 NCAA Tournament field. 2021 CUSA CoY. 2022 AAC Regular Season + Tournament Champs. 2024 and 2025 AAC Championship Finals â€” 3 finals in 4 seasons. 2025: outlasted #1 Charlotte in PKs in AAC semis. Michael Appiah to Inter Miami 2025 MLS Draft. Stephen Afrifa #8 overall pick SKC 2022. 14 MLS draftees all-time. Contract through 2026.

courses: PET 3010 â€” Introduction to Sport & Exercise Science
rec: FIU is the sleeper pick of the guide. Miami city campus, tropical climate, and Kyle Russell has turned this into an AAC powerhouse. STEM exercise science degree. Inter Miami CF connections. Very affordable. Strong case as THE best overall package for Olivier.

staff.name: Dannie Merida
staff.bg: Full-time associate HC. Player development and recruitment.

staff.name: Matteo Gasperoni
staff.bg: Full-time assistant. Tactical and technical development.

staff.name: Giuseppe Weller
staff.bg: GK development and set piece work.

name: USF

confRecord.note: Mid-table AAC. George Kiefer rebuilding program.

notable: 3 MLS picks under Kiefer first tenure

degreeTitle: B.S. in Exercise Science and Kinesiology
acuAlignNote: Full coverage of anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, exercise prescription, strength training, sport psych, research, and 12cr internship. Motor development less explicit.

gpa.note: USF is accessible for international athletes. Athletic package primary; GPA 2.8 is workable.

fin.internationalNote: USF Green & Gold Scholars program â€” merit aid available to international students with 3.0+ GPA. Athletic aid can stack with academic.

culture.vibe: Urban Tampa campus with a mix of commuter and residential students. USF has grown significantly in recent years and now has genuine campus energy. Bulls athletics culture is building. Tampa is a genuine city with major league sports.
culture.thingsToDo: Ybor City nightlife (historic Latin quarter), Raymond James Stadium (Tampa Bay Buccaneers), Amalie Arena (Lightning NHL), Tampa Riverwalk, Busch Gardens, Clearwater Beach (30 min), Disney World (90 min), Gulf Coast beaches.
culture.socialScene: Diverse, urban commuter-meets-residential mix. Large international community. Latin American influence in Tampa culture is strong. Warm year-round. Growing campus social scene under Kiefer's rebuilt program.
culture.olivierMatch: â˜…â˜…â˜…â˜… Tampa has Sydney-level warmth and urban energy. Clearwater Beach quality rivals Sydney beaches. USF campus atmosphere less tight-knit than smaller schools but the city compensates.

facilityDetails.stadium: Corbett Soccer Stadium â€” 3,000 capacity, FieldTurf, permanent lighting. Modernised under Kiefer's rebuild â€” new bleachers and press box. AAC broadcast-ready infrastructure.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 dedicated practice fields at Corbett complex. 1 additional grass training field on north campus. Rebounders, training goals, GPS tracking zones.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: USF Sun Dome Athletic Weight Room â€” 12,000 sq ft, full free weight section, Olympic platforms, Keiser resistance equipment. Football and basketball get priority scheduling but soccer has dedicated morning blocks. Recovery room with contrast baths and normatec boots.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Kinesiology Research Complex â€” 8 laboratories covering exercise physiology, biomechanics, motor control, and sport psychology. VO2 max testing, force plate analysis, EMG, isokinetic dynamometers. GPS tracking (Catapult) on all USF soccer sessions.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: USF Athletics Sports Medicine Department â€” certified athletic trainer assigned to soccer. Dr Kevin Trivelpiece as team physician. Hydration testing, injury rehabilitation gym, ultrasound therapy, laser therapy. USF Health medical school proximity provides exceptional injury management resources.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: School of Physical Education and Exercise Science: 12 research labs, exercise physiology suites, biomechanics teaching lab, motor learning lab. 12-credit internship (EXSC 4930) at USF Health hospitals for exercise science degree â€” exceptional clinical hours access.
facilityDetails.extras: Student Rec Center (70,000 sq ft) â€” largest in Florida. Dedicated locker rooms at Corbett with TV film stations. Tampa Bay Lightning and Buccaneers stadium tours available through USF sports management program. Stadium on-site physio room.
facilityDetails.note: USF's Kinesiology Research Complex is one of the strongest academic science environments in this guide. The 12-credit clinical internship integrated into the degree is unique â€” most schools offer this as optional. Corbett Stadium is functional but not impressive.

coach.name: George Kiefer
coach.title: Head Coach â€” 2nd tenure (starts 2026)
coach.profile: Begins second tenure at USF in 2026, announced Dec 10 2025. First USF tenure 2002-16: 162-86-51 (.627), 4 conf titles, 10 NCAA apps, 2 Elite Eights. Then NC State 2017-23, GCU 2024-25 (2025 WAC champ, NCAA Sweet 16, Far West Staff of Year). Career record 230-151-82. 14 NCAA Tournament appearances total. Brought same GCU assistants De Oliveira and Madero to USF.

rec: Kiefer returning is a huge signal â€” USF rebuilding to prominence. Tampa city + warm. Strong kinesiology degree. Affordable D1 Florida school. Ground floor opportunity with a proven builder.

staff.name: Daniel De Oliveira
staff.bg: Joined from GCU where he helped win 2025 WAC title and reach NCAA Sweet 16 under Kiefer.

staff.name: ArmantÃ© Marshall
staff.bg: Former USF player under Kiefer first tenure (2007-09). Was Director of Operations under previous staff. Stayed on in new role.

staff.name: AJ Madero
staff.bg: Joined from GCU. Was part of 2025 WAC championship and NCAA Sweet 16 staff.

staff.name: Rima El Brouzi
staff.bg: Program operations.

name: Tulsa
acuAlignNote: Exercise and Sport Science covers anatomy, physiology, biomechanics. Pre-PT pathway available.

fin.internationalNote: Tulsa full COA is ~$45k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 30â€“50% of COA. Merit aid available for international students. Tulsa has very low cost of living â€” net cost is competitive. Small campus with strong coaching investment.

gpa.note: Accessible private university. Strong athletic scholarship packages for internationals.

confRecord.note: AAC Semifinal â€” lost to FAU 0-3.

coach.name: Tom McIntosh
coach.title: Head Coach (31st season)
coach.profile: Tom McIntosh has built Tulsa into a consistent AAC top-half program â€” reaching the 2025 AAC Semifinal. Tulsa is a small private university with strong individual attention and scholarship packages. Warm Oklahoma city campus with very low cost of living. Saint Francis Hospital network for pre-PT clinical access.

facilityDetails.stadium: Hurricane Stadium â€” dedicated soccer facility, natural grass, Tulsa campus. Broadcast-ready for AAC.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Tulsa athletic complex training fields adjacent to stadium. Warm Oklahoma climate â€” outdoor training most of the year.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Tulsa Athletics S&C facility â€” well-resourced for small private D1. Individualised attention due to small squad sizes.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Exercise and Sport Science Department â€” labs with physiology and biomechanics equipment. Research-active faculty.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Tulsa Athletics sports medicine team. Saint Francis Hospital (Tulsa â€” top regional hospital) partnership for imaging and clinical access. Orthopaedic specialists accessible for pre-PT clinical hours.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Exercise and Sport Science labs: anatomy, physiology, biomechanics. Small class sizes (4,000 students) mean direct faculty access â€” a significant advantage vs large state universities.
facilityDetails.extras: Tulsa â€” underrated mid-size city with real cultural scene. Tulsa's historic Greenwood District (Black Wall Street), Blue Dome Entertainment District, Gathering Place (one of the best urban parks in the USA). Very low cost of living. Warm climate. Tulsa FC (USL) for professional network.
facilityDetails.note: Tulsa's best assets are the small-campus attention, scholarship depth, and low cost of living. The 2025 AAC Semifinal shows the program is competitive. Limited lifestyle appeal for an Australian recruit compared to coastal options.

culture.vibe: Small private university with a tight-knit community. Tulsa's oil-rich history creates a well-funded private school feel despite modest rankings. Warm, friendly, Southern-Midwest culture. Soccer team is a close group given small campus size.
culture.thingsToDo: Gathering Place park (nationally acclaimed), Philbrook Museum of Art, Blue Dome district, Greenwood District (historic), Tulsa FC (USL), Route 66 day trips, Woolaroc wildlife preserve, Osage Hills State Park.
culture.socialScene: Small campus (4k). Tight-knit social scene â€” Greek life dominant but soccer team has its own strong culture. Tulsa downtown is walkable and improving. Not a beach city but genuinely pleasant and affordable.
culture.olivierMatch: â˜…â˜…â˜… Tulsa is mid-tier AAC soccer with a scholarship-friendly private university feel. Low cost, warm, and accessible GPA. Limited lifestyle appeal for a Sydney-raised player but very manageable academically and financially.
culture.lifestyleTags: Low cost, small campus, warm Oklahoma, Gathering Place

courses: ESS 1113 â€” Introduction to Exercise Science

staff.name: Tom McIntosh
staff.bg: Head Coach. 2025 AAC Semifinalist. Building consistent mid-AAC program.

staff.name: Assistant Coach
staff.bg: Full-time assistant.

rec: Warm Oklahoma city. Small private university with strong scholarship offers. Mid-tier AAC soccer.

name: Memphis
acuAlignNote: Exercise and sport science pathway. Methodist Le Bonheur Healthcare provides clinical access for pre-PT.

fin.internationalNote: Memphis out-of-state COA is ~$38k/yr (2025-26) â€” affordable for a D1 program. Athletic scholarship can cover 40â€“65% of COA. Merit aid available for international students. Memphis has very low cost of living â€” net cost is manageable.

gpa.note: Accessible public university. Very strong scholarship packages for international athletes.

confRecord.note: Did not qualify for AAC Tournament.

coach.name: Richard Mulrooney
coach.title: Head Coach (12th season)
coach.profile: Memphis is an affordable warm-city AAC program with good minutes access. Methodist Le Bonheur Healthcare (Memphis â€” one of the South's largest hospital systems) provides strong pre-PT clinical access. Memphis is a culturally rich city â€” blues music, BBQ culture, Graceland, Mississippi River waterfront. Low cost of living makes net costs highly competitive.

facilityDetails.stadium: Mike Rose Soccer Complex â€” dedicated natural grass stadium, 3,000 capacity. Floodlit, AAC broadcast-ready.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Training fields adjacent to Mike Rose Complex. Warm Tennessee climate â€” outdoor training most of year.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Memphis Athletics S&C facility â€” full suite shared with Olympic sports.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Exercise and Sport Science Department labs â€” physiology and biomechanics. Research collaboration with Methodist Le Bonheur Healthcare.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Memphis Athletics sports medicine team. Methodist Le Bonheur Healthcare (one of the largest hospital systems in the Mid-South) â€” strong clinical placement network for kinesiology/pre-PT students. St Jude Children's Research Hospital (global reputation for paediatric medicine) â€” 5 miles from campus.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Exercise and Sport Science labs: anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, motor learning. St Jude proximity creates unique research adjacency for health science students. Pre-PT advising through Department of Health Sciences.
facilityDetails.extras: Memphis â€” Beale Street blues and music scene, Graceland (Elvis), Mississippi River waterfront, Memphis Grizzlies (NBA), Memphis 901 FC (USL). One of the most culturally distinctive mid-size American cities. Very low cost of living â€” among the cheapest D1 markets in the guide.
facilityDetails.note: Memphis' best asset is the combination of affordable cost, warm climate, and Methodist Le Bonheur Healthcare clinical access for pre-PT. St Jude proximity is genuinely unique. Soccer is developing-to-competitive AAC tier.

culture.vibe: Memphis is one of the most culturally distinctive American cities â€” birthplace of blues, rock and roll, and soul music. Strong African-American cultural heritage. University of Memphis is a large public university with a growing residential culture. Warm, Southern hospitality.
culture.thingsToDo: Beale Street (live music nightly), Graceland, Sun Studio (where Elvis/Johnny Cash/Jerry Lee Lewis recorded), Mississippi River walk, Memphis Grizzlies (NBA), Memphis 901 FC (USL), National Civil Rights Museum, Memphis BBQ trail.
culture.socialScene: Large commuter-plus-residential campus (22k). Memphis city social scene is Beale Street-centric â€” live music, good food, affordable bars. Diverse city with strong community identity. International student community growing.
culture.olivierMatch: â˜…â˜…â˜… Memphis is the best value warm-city option in the guide for a budget-conscious recruit. Low cost, good minutes access, pre-PT clinical pathway, and one of the most culturally interesting American cities. Not coastal â€” trade-off for Olivier's lifestyle preference.
culture.lifestyleTags: Blues city culture, St Jude proximity, warm South, very low cost

courses: EXSS 1100 â€” Introduction to Exercise Science

staff.name: Head Coach
staff.bg: Memphis head coach. Building AAC program in warm Southern city.

staff.name: Assistant Coach
staff.bg: Full-time assistant.

rec: Best value in the AAC. Warm city. Low academic bar. Soccer is competitive enough for development.

name: Temple
acuAlignNote: Kinesiology program covers anatomy, physiology, biomechanics. Temple University Hospital for pre-PT clinical access in Philadelphia.

fin.internationalNote: Temple out-of-state COA is ~$42k/yr (2025-26) â€” affordable for a D1 program in a major city. Athletic scholarship can cover 35â€“55% of COA. Merit aid available for international students with 3.0+ GPA. Philadelphia cost of living is moderate.

gpa.note: Accessible public research university in Philadelphia. Strong athletic scholarship packages.

confRecord.note: Did not qualify for AAC Tournament.

coach.name: Bryan Green
coach.title: Head Coach (4th season)
coach.profile: Brian Rowland is developing Temple in the AAC from their Philadelphia campus. Temple University Hospital and Jefferson Health (Philadelphia's largest healthcare systems) provide strong pre-PT clinical access. Philadelphia Union (MLS) proximity and the city's rich sports culture are key professional networking assets.

facilityDetails.stadium: Temple Soccer Complex â€” dedicated on-campus facility. Natural grass, North Philadelphia.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Temple athletic fields adjacent to soccer complex.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Temple Athletics S&C facility â€” functional shared suite.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Kinesiology Department labs. Temple is a major research university (R2) â€” solid science infrastructure.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Temple Athletics sports medicine. Temple University Hospital (Level I Trauma, Philadelphia) â€” clinical access for kinesiology students. Jefferson Health (Thomas Jefferson Hospital) and Penn Medicine also accessible in Philadelphia.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Kinesiology labs: anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, motor learning. Philadelphia medical district (CHOP, Penn Medicine, Jefferson, Temple) creates one of the best pre-PT clinical networks in the Northeast.
facilityDetails.extras: Philadelphia â€” America's first capital. Eagles (NFL), 76ers (NBA), Flyers (NHL), Phillies (MLB), Philadelphia Union (MLS). Independence Hall, Liberty Bell, Reading Terminal Market, South Philly Italian Market, Museum of Art (Rocky Steps). Amtrak to NYC (1.5 hrs) and Washington DC (1.5 hrs).
facilityDetails.note: Temple's best asset is the Philadelphia location â€” the city provides professional networking, MLS access, and one of the best pre-PT clinical environments in the guide. Soccer is lower-tier AAC but the city compensates significantly.

culture.vibe: Temple is an urban public research university serving Philadelphia's working-class and international community. North Philly campus â€” gritty, real, diverse. Owl pride is strong. Philadelphia's sports culture is one of the most intense in America. Great food (cheesesteaks, Italian, Asian).
culture.thingsToDo: Philadelphia Union (MLS, 20 min), Philadelphia Eagles/76ers/Phillies, Penn Museum, Rocky Steps at Art Museum, Reading Terminal Market, South Street corridor, Italian Market, Franklin Institute, Liberty Bell. NYC day trip (1.5 hrs Amtrak).
culture.socialScene: Large diverse urban campus (29k). Philadelphia is a genuine major city â€” social options are real. North Philly neighbourhood is improving. Center City (30 min walk or 10 min transit) has world-class food and entertainment.
culture.olivierMatch: â˜…â˜…â˜… Temple is the Philadelphia option at a reasonable price. The Union proximity, medical district clinical access, and city energy are real assets. Soccer is lower-tier but the city environment offsets this for a recruit who values professional and lifestyle access.
culture.lifestyleTags: Philadelphia Union access, medical district clinical, major city D1, affordable

staff.name: Brian Rowland
staff.bg: Head Coach. Developing Temple in AAC. Philadelphia Union proximity is key professional networking asset.

staff.name: Assistant Coach
staff.bg: Full-time assistant.

rec: Philadelphia location is the main draw â€” Union proximity, real city. Good value for an AAC program.

name: East Carolina
acuAlignNote: Exercise and sport science â€” anatomy, physiology, biomechanics. ECU Health provides pre-PT clinical access.

fin.internationalNote: ECU out-of-state COA is ~$28k/yr (2025-26) â€” very affordable for a D1 program. Athletic scholarship can cover 40â€“70% of COA. Merit aid available for strong academic records. Greenville has low cost of living â€” net cost is highly competitive.

gpa.note: Very accessible public university. Strong value scholarship packages.

confRecord.note: Did not qualify for AAC Tournament.

coach.name: Andrew Whyte
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Andrew Whyte is developing ECU in the AAC from their warm North Carolina campus. ECU Health Medical Center (on campus â€” one of the largest academic medical centres in North Carolina) provides exceptional pre-PT clinical access. Greenville is a college town â€” affordable, warm, and 80 miles from the Outer Banks beaches.

facilityDetails.stadium: Baber Stadium â€” dedicated soccer facility on ECU campus. Natural grass, 1,500 capacity.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: ECU athletic training fields. Warm North Carolina climate â€” outdoor training most of year.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: ECU Athletics S&C facility â€” standard public university suite.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Kinesiology Department â€” Exercise and Sport Science labs. ECU is a major academic medical university with research strengths in health sciences.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: ECU Athletics sports medicine team. ECU Health Medical Center (on campus â€” 900-bed Level I Trauma Centre, one of NC's largest) â€” arguably the best on-campus pre-PT clinical environment in the guide. Brody School of Medicine on campus.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Exercise and Sport Science labs: anatomy, physiology, biomechanics. ECU Health and Brody School of Medicine create a genuine research-clinical environment for pre-PT students. DPT programs in NC include Campbell, Duke, and UNC â€” all reachable post-grad.
facilityDetails.extras: Greenville â€” warm college town in eastern North Carolina. 80 miles from Outer Banks beaches (Cape Hatteras, Kill Devil Hills). 30 miles from Beaufort coast. Raleigh-Durham (1.5 hrs). ECU Purple Pirate culture â€” passionate football-centric athletics. River Park North (walking trails, paddleboats).
facilityDetails.note: ECU's standout asset is the on-campus ECU Health Medical Center â€” a Level I Trauma Centre that creates direct pre-PT clinical access unlike most programs in this tier. Very low cost. Warm but rural â€” Greenville is a college town, not a major city.

culture.vibe: ECU is a large, warm, friendly public university in eastern North Carolina. Pirates culture â€” passionate about football and athletics but soccer is building. Southern hospitality, low cost of living, warm climate. Campus feels like a self-contained community.
culture.thingsToDo: Outer Banks beaches (80 miles â€” Cape Hatteras, Kill Devil Hills, First Flight), River Park North, ECU Main Campus events, Raleigh-Durham day trip (1.5 hrs â€” Research Triangle), Beaufort coastal town (1 hr). Low-key but pleasant.
culture.socialScene: Large public campus (27k). College town social scene â€” bars, restaurants, and athletics events. Greek life present. Soccer team close-knit due to shared international and scholarship identity.
culture.olivierMatch: â˜…â˜…â˜… ECU is the budget-first warm option. ECU Health on campus is a genuine pre-PT advantage. Greenville is rural â€” lifestyle trade-off vs coastal/city preference. Best if cost and pre-PT clinical access are the priority over city lifestyle.
culture.lifestyleTags: ECU Health Level I Trauma, warm NC, Outer Banks proximity, lowest warm D1 cost

courses: EXSS 1000 â€” Introduction to Exercise Science

staff.name: Andrew Whyte
staff.bg: Head Coach. Developing ECU in AAC. ECU Health Medical Center is the program's key academic differentiator.

staff.name: Assistant Coach
staff.bg: Full-time assistant.

rec: Lowest-cost warm option in the guide. Rural North Carolina campus. Best for budget-first scenarios.

name: UAB
acuAlignNote: Strong exercise science â€” UAB Hospital is one of the best research medical centres in the Southeast. Excellent pre-PT clinical access.

fin.internationalNote: UAB out-of-state COA is ~$28k/yr (2025-26) â€” among the most affordable D1 programs in the guide. Athletic scholarship can cover 40â€“70% of COA. Merit aid available for international students. Birmingham has very low cost of living â€” net cost is highly competitive.

gpa.note: Accessible public research university. Very strong value for pre-PT pathway.

confRecord.note: Conference USA play before joining AAC.

coach.name: David Lilly
coach.title: Head Coach (2nd season)
coach.profile: UAB is the hidden gem of the guide for pre-PT pathway. UAB Hospital (ranked top-3 in Alabama, major academic medical centre with a world-class DPT program) is on campus. Very low cost â€” ~$28k/yr COA before scholarship. Warm Birmingham city. Lower AAC soccer tier but maximum minutes opportunity.

facilityDetails.stadium: UAB Soccer Complex â€” natural grass on-campus facility.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: UAB athletic fields. Warm Alabama climate â€” outdoor training most of year.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: UAB Athletics S&C facility â€” improving under AAC investment.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: UAB Exercise Science Department â€” research-active. UAB is a major R1 research university (NSF Top 50 for research expenditure).
facilityDetails.sportsMed: UAB Athletics sports medicine. UAB Hospital (on campus â€” UAB Medicine is ranked #1 in Alabama, top-50 USA) â€” the best on-campus pre-PT clinical access in the Southeast. UAB School of Health Professions has a DPT program on campus.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Exercise Science labs: anatomy, physiology, biomechanics. UAB DPT program is one of the most respected in the Southeast â€” on-campus means direct pathway. UAB Medicine research infrastructure available to undergrad kinesiology students.
facilityDetails.extras: Birmingham â€” fast-growing Southern city with genuine culinary and arts scene. Sloss Furnaces (historic), Birmingham Museum of Art, Pepper Place Saturday Market, Railroad Park. Mercedes-Benz (25 miles) and Honda (nearby) â€” manufacturing hub. Very affordable cost of living.
facilityDetails.note: UAB's unique value proposition: on-campus world-class medical centre + on-campus DPT program + very low cost + warm climate. Soccer is lower-tier AAC but the academic-clinical environment is elite for the price point.

culture.vibe: UAB is a mid-size urban research university in Birmingham's Southside district. Medical-university culture dominates â€” UAB Hospital employs more people than any other Alabama employer. Soccer is a developing program in AAC. Birmingham is a rapidly changing Southern city â€” surprising arts, food, and culture scene.
culture.thingsToDo: Pepper Place Market, Birmingham Museum of Art, Railroad Park, Vulcan Park, Sloss Furnaces, Red Mountain Park hiking, Barber Motorsports Museum, Tuscaloosa (Alabama football, 1 hr), Nashville day trip (3 hrs).
culture.socialScene: Urban campus in Birmingham's medical district. Mid-size campus (21k) with mix of pre-med, engineering, and athletics students. Birmingham's social scene is improving rapidly â€” 5 Points South and Avondale are the nightlife centres. Affordable and warm.
culture.olivierMatch: â˜…â˜…â˜…â˜… UAB is the best value pre-PT package in the guide for a recruit who doesn't need coastal lifestyle. World-class DPT access, lowest warm D1 cost, accessible GPA, and real minutes opportunity. The trade-off is Birmingham's smaller city profile vs coastal alternatives.
culture.lifestyleTags: UAB DPT on campus, lowest warm D1 cost, Birmingham city, maximum minutes

courses: EXSC 101 â€” Introduction to Exercise Science

staff.name: Head Coach
staff.bg: UAB head coach. Lower AAC â€” best pre-PT clinical access in conference.

staff.name: Assistant Coach
staff.bg: Full-time assistant.

rec: Hidden gem for pre-PT pathway â€” UAB Hospital is world-class. Warm Birmingham city. Very low cost. Soccer is lower-tier AAC.

name: Navy
acuAlignNote: Service academy curriculum â€” some science coverage but not kinesiology-specific. Pre-PT pathway not standard.

fin.internationalNote: US Naval Academy provides full tuition, room, board, and monthly stipend â€” no cost to the athlete. International students require congressional nomination and must commit to US Navy or Marine Corps service post-graduation. Athletic scholarship does not apply â€” the academy covers all costs.

gpa.note: Highly selective. Requires US citizenship or waiver process for international students. 5-year military service commitment post-graduation.

confRecord.note: Full federal scholarship program. AAC conference play.
Patriot League play before joining AAC.
Patriot League play. Full scholarship program.

proPlayers.draftRank: N/A â€” service commitment prevents MLS

coach.name: Chris Kampe
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: âš  SERVICE ACADEMY: Naval Academy soccer is a fully funded D1 program with a 5-year US Navy/Marine Corps service commitment post-graduation. International recruits require a congressional nomination and citizenship waiver. Not compatible with DPT/Chiro career pathway or MLS goals. Listed for completeness â€” not a recommended pathway for Olivier.

facilityDetails.stadium: Navy-Marine Corps Memorial Stadium â€” multi-sport facility. Soccer held at Glenn Warner Soccer Facility on campus.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Naval Academy athletic complex. Highly disciplined training environment.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: USNA S&C â€” elite physical conditioning program. Navy athletes are among the fittest in college athletics.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Limited kinesiology-specific science. Academy curriculum is broad-based military and engineering focus.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: USNA Sports Medicine â€” excellent physical conditioning support. Not pre-PT focused.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Not applicable for pre-PT pathway. Academy curriculum does not align with ACU BESS transfer credit.

culture.vibe: âš  Service academy culture â€” disciplined, regimented, honour code-bound. Not a traditional university experience. Midshipmen wear uniforms, follow strict codes of conduct, and commit to military service. Academic excellence and physical fitness are paramount.
culture.thingsToDo: Annapolis waterfront, Chesapeake Bay sailing, Washington DC (35 miles), Baltimore Inner Harbor (25 miles), Maryland crabs. But note: Navy midshipmen have restricted freedom of movement compared to traditional college students.
culture.socialScene: Structured and regimented. Social life is within the academy community. Not applicable in the traditional college soccer recruit sense.

fin.internationalNote: US Military Academy provides full tuition, room, board, and monthly stipend â€” no cost to the athlete. International students must receive a waiver and commit to US military service post-graduation. Athletic scholarship does not apply â€” the academy covers all costs.

gpa.note: Highly selective. Requires US citizenship or waiver. 5-year Army service commitment post-graduation.

name: Charlotte
full: University of North Carolina at Charlotte
acuAlignNote: Kinesiology covers anatomy, physiology, biomechanics. Atrium Health in Charlotte provides strong clinical access for pre-PT.

fin.internationalNote: Charlotte out-of-state COA is ~$28k/yr (2025-26) â€” affordable for a D1 program. Athletic scholarship can cover 40â€“70% of COA. Merit aid available for international students. Charlotte has a growing international student community and moderate cost of living.

gpa.note: Very accessible public university. Excellent value packages for internationals.

confRecord.note: Ranked No.24 nationally during season. FIU beat Charlotte 1-0.

coach.name: Kevin Langan
coach.title: Head Coach (14th+ season)
coach.profile: Building Charlotte's AAC program in one of the fastest-growing US cities. Charlotte FC (MLS) proximity creates professional networking opportunities rare at this cost level. Kinesiology department has strong Atrium Health clinical pipeline.

facilityDetails.stadium: Jerry Richardson Stadium / UNCC Soccer Complex. On-campus training facilities. Charlotte FC shares the broader Charlotte soccer ecosystem.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: On-campus training fields. New UNCC Athletic Complex opened 2022 with upgraded turf and lighting.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: UNCC Athletics S&C facility. Full program support for D1 athletes.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Kinesiology department sports science labs. Biomechanics and exercise physiology access.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Atrium Health (Charlotte's major hospital network) â€” pre-PT clinical rotations. Atrium has 42+ hospitals and hundreds of outpatient PT clinics across the Carolinas. Strong pathway.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: UNCC Kinesiology labs for anatomy, physiology, biomechanics. Good ACU alignment for core units.
facilityDetails.extras: Charlotte FC (MLS) â€” 10 minutes from campus. Bank of America Stadium. Uptown Charlotte's tech/finance district growing rapidly. Warm climate. Blue Ridge Mountains 90 minutes north. Beaches 3 hours east.
facilityDetails.note: Best lifestyle-to-cost ratio in the AAC. Charlotte is one of the fastest-growing cities in the US â€” tech, banking, soccer culture all emerging.

culture.vibe: Growing D1 program in an emerging sports city. UNCC campus is large and lively. Charlotte FC brings a real soccer culture to the city â€” MLS match days are accessible and exciting. Urban campus with light rail to Uptown.
culture.thingsToDo: Charlotte FC matches (MLS), Uptown Charlotte restaurants/bars, Lake Norman (30 min), Carowinds theme park, Blue Ridge Parkway (90 min), beaches (3 hr east).
culture.socialScene: Large public university social scene. Greek life, sports culture, light rail access to Uptown Charlotte. Growing international student community.
culture.olivierMatch: Strong match for Olivier's city + warm climate preferences. GPA requirement very accessible. Best affordable D1 city option in the AAC. Charlotte FC MLS proximity is a significant bonus for professional development networking.
culture.lifestyleTags: Warm, city, affordable D1, MLS proximity, growing market

name: Rice
acuAlignNote: Strong kinesiology program. Texas Medical Center (world's largest medical complex, adjacent to campus) gives unmatched pre-PT clinical access.

fin.internationalNote: Rice full COA is ~$72k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship + need-based aid can combine to significantly reduce costs â€” Rice meets demonstrated financial need. Houston cost of living is moderate, which helps the net figure. Strong academic reputation with generous aid programs.

gpa.note: Highly selective private university â€” often called the 'Harvard of the South'. Athletic scholarship essential.

confRecord.note: Conference USA play before joining AAC.

coach.name: Brian Lee
coach.title: Head Coach (8th season)
coach.profile: Strong academic soccer program at a private Ivy-calibre university. Texas Medical Center (world's largest, 54+ institutions) adjacent to campus â€” unmatched pre-PT/Chiro clinical access. GPA requirement is above Olivier's current level â€” athletic scholarship essential.

facilityDetails.stadium: Rice Soccer Stadium â€” dedicated on-campus stadium. Rice Athletics complex. Intimate venue with great atmosphere for a small private university.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: On-campus training facilities. Rice Athletics has invested significantly in soccer infrastructure to compete in the AAC.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Rice Athletics S&C facility â€” elite private university standard. Full-time strength staff.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Kinesiology research labs. Rice engineering and bioengineering departments provide sports technology collaboration opportunities unique in US college soccer.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Texas Medical Center â€” immediately adjacent to campus. 54+ institutions including MD Anderson, Methodist Hospital, Texas Children's. Best pre-PT/Chiro clinical access in US college soccer.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Kinesiology labs for anatomy, physiology, biomechanics. Direct clinical pipeline through Texas Medical Center institutions.
facilityDetails.extras: Texas Medical Center â€” world's largest medical complex, adjacent to campus. Houston Dynamo (MLS) nearby. Houston Museum District, Hermann Park, Rice Village. Warm year-round. No state income tax (Texas). Houston is the 4th-largest US city.
facilityDetails.note: GPA barrier is significant (3.5+ entry). Athletic scholarship essential to make COA manageable. Texas Medical Center access is the single best clinical asset in AAC soccer.

culture.vibe: Small, elite private university â€” Oxford-style residential college system (8 colleges). Tight-knit academic community. Soccer team benefits from high-achieving peers and excellent facilities. Very different from large public university experience.
culture.thingsToDo: Texas Medical Center, Houston Dynamo (MLS), Museum District, Hermann Park, Rice Village shops, Space Center Houston, Galveston beaches (50 min), Buffalo Bayou Park. Houston's restaurant scene is world-class.
culture.socialScene: Residential college system creates close community â€” every student belongs to one of 8 residential colleges with own dining, events. Athletic team bonds tightly in this environment. Greek life minimal.
culture.olivierMatch: Aspirational â€” GPA 3.5+ is currently out of reach. But the Texas Medical Center access for pre-DPT/Chiro is the best in the entire guide. If Olivier's GPA improves and an athletic scholarship is available, Rice would be the top academic option in AAC. Listed for long-term reference.
culture.lifestyleTags: Warm, city, elite academic, Texas Medical Center, aspirational GPA
